initial_program:
% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one output colour per cell (will be enforced by the cardinality rule)

% Explicit transformations for the changed cells
output(1,5,3) :- input(2,4,3).
output(2,5,3) :- input(2,4,3).
output(2,6,3) :- input(2,4,3).
output(2,7,2) :- input(2,5,2).
output(2,4,0) :- true.

% Constraints to forbid the copy colour for the changed cells
:- not(output(1,5,0)).
:- not(output(2,5,2)).
:- not(output(2,6,0)).
:- not(output(2,7,0)).
:- not(output(2,4,3)).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

% Grid dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% Exactly one output colour per cell (will be enforced by the cardinality rule)

% Explicit transformations for the changed cells
output(1,5,3) :- input(2,4,3).
output(2,5,3) :- input(2,4,3).
output(2,6,3) :- input(2,4,3).
output(2,7,2) :- input(2,5,2).
output(2,4,0) :- true.

:- not(output(1,5,0)), not(output(2,5,2)), not(output(2,6,0)), not(output(2,7,0)), not(output(2,4,3)).

% Exactly one output colour per cell
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).
